one_time_description: Government of Canada forgave the remaining balance of the BC Treaty negotiation loan payable of $179,200
economic_notes: First Nation has investments in multiple government business enterprises and partnerships including oil and gas exploration (ADK Petroleum), fuel sales (Liard Fuel Centre), construction and transportation (Beaver Enterprises), camps and catering (Acho Camps & Catering), charter aircraft landing rights (Deh Cho Air), residential housing (Acho Real Estate), and pipeline infrastructure (Shiha Energy Transmission). Most enterprises are reporting losses. Unnamed Benefit Agreement provides $1,000,000 over ten years.
notes: Fiscal year is 2020, not 2024. Audit is qualified because GBEs/partnerships (ADK Holdings Ltd.) have a May 31 year-end that does not coincide with the First Nation's March 31 year-end; transactions between June 1, 2019 and March 31, 2020 were not recorded. GBEs report under ASPE rather than IFRS as required by PSAB. Equity losses of $940,438 significantly impacted the annual surplus resulting in a deficit of $848,237. BC Treaty negotiation loan of $179,200 was forgiven during the year. The nation is economically dependent on Government of Canada and GNWT funding. No schedule of remuneration was included in the provided text. Contributions receivable of $537,448 not included in accounts receivable figure above. total_government_transfers includes Government of Canada $1,319,954 + GNWT $543,310 + Dehcho First Nation $58,403 + BC Treaty Commission $122,637 = $2,044,304; osr = $2,347,586 - $2,044,304 = $303,282 (resource royalties $120,605 + other income $182,677).
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
